title: ARCHON_PRIME Implementation Guide Schema V2
description: Validates the structure of Implementation Guide artifacts. V2 enforces deterministic build sequencing, header injection, spec compliance verification, analog reconciliation, enhancement integration gates, artifact isolation, and architecture drift detection. A valid implementation guide must be present before any code generation begins.

guide_identity.description: Artifact identity block. Must be completed before any other section.

source_specification.description: Artifact ID of the Design Specification (SPEC-NNNN) this guide implements. A guide without a locked or approved source spec is invalid.

design_origin.description: Artifact ID of the Design Specification.

overview.description: Plain-language statement of what this guide does and what it produces.

prerequisites.description: All conditions that must be true before this guide's implementation sequence may begin.

blocking.description: If true, implementation cannot begin until this prerequisite is satisfied.

deterministic_build_sequence.description: The ordered, deterministic sequence of build operations. Every step is explicit. No step may be reordered without Architect authorization. This is the primary enforcement mechanism against implementation drift.

step_id.description: Build step identifier. Must be unique and ordered.

action.description: Concrete action to execute in this step.

blocking.description: If true, failure in this step halts the entire build.

validation_required.description: Whether architecture validation must pass before this step is considered complete.

depends_on.description: Step IDs that must complete successfully before this step executes.

produces.description: The artifact, directory, or state this step produces.

reorder_rule.description: Whether build steps may be reordered. Default: prohibited.

header_injection_rules.description: Rules governing the injection and validation of machine-readable module headers. Every runtime module must receive a valid header. Headers must match canonical registry values. Modules without valid headers fail architecture validation.

header_source.description: Authoritative source for header field values. Must be canonical_module_registry for spec-controlled implementations.

on_missing_header.description: What happens when a runtime module lacks a header.

on_registry_mismatch.description: What happens when a header field value does not match the canonical registry.

spec_compliance_checks.description: Checks that verify each implemented module conforms to the source Design Specification. Run at module generation time and again at architecture validation.

failure_disposition.description: What happens when this check fails.

module_generation_contract.description: Rules that govern the generation of every module. A module may only be generated if it satisfies all contract conditions. This is a pre-generation gate, not a post-generation audit.

pre_generation_conditions.description: All conditions must be true before a module generation is permitted.

post_generation_verification.description: Checks to run immediately after a module is generated.

analog_reconciliation_process.description: Process for detecting and reconciling analog implementations — modules present in the repository that perform spec-defined functions but were not generated from the spec. Analog implementations must be detected and classified; they may not persist as UNKNOWN_MODULE indefinitely.

detection_method.description: How analog implementations are detected (e.g., architecture validation scan).

requires_architect_decision.description: Whether this step requires an explicit Architect decision before proceeding.

disposition_options.description: The possible outcomes of analog reconciliation.

unresolved_analog_rule.description: What happens to an analog that has not been reconciled after a defined period. Must specify disposition (e.g., 'flagged as UNKNOWN_MODULE and escalated to Architect').

enhancement_integration_rules.description: Rules governing enhancement module integration. Enhancements may not bypass the spec update process. This rule has no exceptions unless the Architect explicitly authorizes one.

integration_workflow.description: Ordered workflow steps. All steps must complete before an enhancement is integrated.

required_output.description: What artifact or decision must exist before the next step begins.

pre_integration_gate.description: All conditions must be true before integration is permitted.

bypassable.description: Whether the enhancement integration workflow may be bypassed.

bypass_authority.description: Who may authorize a bypass. Should be 'Architect' only.

artifact_directory_rules.description: Rules enforcing isolation between artifact surfaces. Export artifacts must never reside in runtime directories. Runtime code must never reside in artifact archive, design, audit, or test directories.

violation_disposition.description: Default action when an artifact directory isolation rule is violated.

quarantine_directory.description: If quarantine is used, the path where misplaced modules are moved pending review.

drift_detection_audit.description: Defines the automated drift detection checks. Drift detection identifies deviations between the repo state and the canonical spec. Must run before enhancement integration, release builds, and at any architecture validation gate.

report_field.description: Field in architecture_validation_report.json where findings are recorded.

blocking.description: Whether a finding in this check blocks pipeline progress.

audit_trigger_conditions.description: Conditions that automatically trigger a drift detection audit.

output_path.description: Path relative to repository root where the audit report is written.

architecture_validation_gate.description: Defines the validation gate that controls pipeline progression. Architecture validation must pass before gated stages may proceed. Failure halts the pipeline.

gate_rule.description: The rule that governs gate behavior. Must be: 'architecture_valid == true is required before any gated stage may proceed'.
gate_rule.const: architecture_valid == true is required before any gated stage may proceed.

gated_stages.description: Pipeline stages that cannot proceed until the validation gate passes.

validator_module.description: Definition of the canonical architecture validator module.

canonical_path.description: Canonical path of the validator module.

input_sources.description: Artifacts or data sources the validator loads.

validation_checks.description: Ordered list of checks the validator performs.

stages.description: Ordered implementation stages. Each stage contains the specific implementation obligations for a subset of modules.

header_required.description: Whether this module requires a header injection. Default true for all runtime modules.

entry_condition.description: What must be true before this stage begins.

exit_condition.description: What must be true before the next stage begins.

validation_gate_required.description: Whether architecture validation must pass before this stage's exit condition is satisfied.

governance_enforcement_points.description: Governance rules that are enforced at specific points during implementation.

ambiguity_log.description: Unresolved ambiguities in the source Design Specification. Each must include a recommendation and escalation status. Blocking ambiguities must be resolved before implementation of the affected module may begin.

blocking_module.description: Module ID whose implementation is blocked by this ambiguity.

meta_rules.description: Schema-level enforcement rules. Apply to any artifact validated against this schema.

rules.rule: Spec Before Code: design_spec_complete and implementation_guide_complete must both be true before any module generation begins.
Enhancements must complete the enhancement_integration_workflow (including spec and guide updates) before any code is committed.
Canonical Identity: any module not matching the canonical registry is blocked from integration until classified and reconciled.
Validation Gate: architecture_valid must be true before any gated pipeline stage proceeds.
Directory Integrity: runtime modules may only exist in directories classified as runtime_surface.
